requirements.title: Memory Allocator Interface
requirements.description: The memory module shall define an abstract allocator API with Get, Update, and Put operations.
requirements.rationale: An abstract allocator interface decouples data structures from a specific allocation strategy, enabling the integrator to provide pool-based, slab, or custom allocators suited to their target's memory constraints.

requirements.title: Allocator Pointer API Dependency
requirements.description: The memory allocator root shall hold an injected pointer API for internal memory operations.
requirements.rationale: Injecting the pointer API ensures the allocator can perform type-safe copy and reset operations on allocated blocks without depending on a specific pointer implementation.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Initialization
requirements.description: The block allocator shall initialize from caller-provided backing memory, metadata array, type size, alignment, and length.
requirements.rationale: Initialization from caller-provided memory ensures the block allocator operates without dynamic allocation and that all backing storage is under integrator control.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Get
requirements.description: The block allocator shall allocate a block of the configured element size in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: O(1) allocation time guarantees deterministic performance, which is essential for real-time embedded applications with strict timing budgets.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Free Block Reuse
requirements.description: The block allocator shall reuse freed blocks via a LIFO free list when available.
requirements.rationale: LIFO free-list reuse maximizes block utilization without fragmentation, keeping the allocator's memory footprint bounded to the initial caller-provided pool.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Put
requirements.description: The block allocator shall free a previously allocated block in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: O(1) deallocation time guarantees deterministic performance and complements the O(1) allocation guarantee.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Double Free Rejection
requirements.description: The block allocator shall reject double frees with an error status.
requirements.rationale: Detecting double frees at runtime prevents free-list corruption that would otherwise cause silent data corruption or unbounded allocation behavior.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Invalid Address Rejection
requirements.description: The block allocator shall reject frees of invalid or misaligned addresses with an error status.
requirements.rationale: Rejecting invalid or misaligned addresses prevents corruption of the free list and ensures that only addresses within the original backing memory are returned to the pool.

requirements.title: Block Allocator Update
requirements.description: The block allocator shall adjust a descriptor's size in-place up to the configured element size.
requirements.rationale: In-place size adjustment allows a descriptor to shrink or grow within its fixed block without triggering a new allocation or data copy, which is useful for variable-length payloads within fixed-size slots.

requirements.title: Block Allocator No Memory Move
requirements.description: The block allocator Update shall not move memory when adjusting descriptor size.
requirements.rationale: Guaranteeing no memory movement during Update ensures that existing pointers to the block remain valid, which is critical for data structures that hold references to allocated blocks.
